focus: [D3-A4/D3] ambiguous/multi-axis prompt resolution undefined; weighted hub scoring with ambiguity delta, tie-breaks, and ordered mode bundles are missing from sk-design routing

graphEvents.label: How should the sk-design hub resolve prompts where multiple design modes score near each other or represent separate axes?
The sk-design hub has prose for smallest useful mode and mode pairing, but no weights, ambiguity delta, tie-breaks, compatibility rules, or router return shape
Existing router-replay keeps near-tied intents and unions resources, but parent design routing needs primary mode, ordered roles, and defer reasons
Skill-benchmark modePrecision is intentionally advisory and single-mode, so wrong parent mode selection can stay outside weighted gating
Build work requires interface plus foundations and optional support modes, but the route result cannot currently express a required ordered bundle
A parent design hub router must deterministically return exactly one of single mode, ordered mode bundle, or deferReason, with traceable scores and tie-breaks

label: sk-design hub lacks machine-readable ambiguity policy
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE on fixture corpus; ADVISORY for open-ended live prompts
recommendation: Add routerPolicy/routerSignals with defaultMode, modeHintOverride, ambiguityDelta, maxBundleModes, tieBreakOrder, and explicit outcomes single|orderedBundle|defer.

label: router-replay near-tie handling is useful but not enough for parent hub mode bundles
recommendation: Add hub-router-replay adapter that turns weighted mode scores into primaryMode, ordered workflowModes with roles, routeTrace, and deferReason.

label: current modePrecision cannot enforce sk-design mode-bundle correctness
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE once fixtures carry expectedModeBundle gold
recommendation: Add expectedModeBundle to hub-router fixtures and score primary mode, order, recall, unexpected-mode penalty, and defer correctness as a gated hub-routing dimension.

label: build bundle requirements are loading/proof rules, not first-class router output
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for load/proof fields; ADVISORY for final design quality
recommendation: Make route.workflowModes[] the source for required context manifest rows and source-proof checks before any READY or benchmark pass claim.

label: A parent design hub router must deterministically return single mode, ordered mode bundle, or deferReason with traceable scores and tie-breaks

direction: Treat modePrecision advisory signal as sufficient enforcement for sk-design hub routing
reason: The benchmark intentionally keeps modePrecision outside dimensionScores and the weighted aggregate, so it can report but not fail wrong parent mode selection.
